_meta.description: Sample skill catalog for the DevOps agent, each skill carrying SkillNet's five quality dimensions (eval_safety, eval_completeness, eval_executability, eval_maintainability, eval_cost_awareness) in [0,1] plus Toolshed retrieval hooks (description, synthetic_queries, key_topics). Ratings are illustrative and would in production come from an independent SkillNet-style evaluator, never self-reported. Two skills are deliberately seeded low-quality to exercise the gate: 'unsafe-shell-runner' (eval_safety=0, hard-excluded) and 'flaky-scraper-skill' (low composite, soft-excluded).
_meta.source: Agentic Graph RAG Ch6 — Skills: The Judgment Layer / SkillNet five-dimension framework (Table 6-1)

skills.name: safe-readonly-sql-query
skills.description: Run an analytical SQL query against a read-only replica: check the table exists first, use a read-only connection, limit result sets, and format output as markdown.
synthetic_queries: query the database for slow requests
read metrics from the reporting table

skills.name: latency-investigation-runbook
skills.description: Encodes the correct sequence for investigating a service latency spike: pull p99 metrics, check upstream dependency saturation, correlate with recent deploys, then form a hypothesis before acting.
synthetic_queries: why is the checkout api slow
trace a latency incident to root cause

skills.name: log-triage-error-patterns
skills.description: Search service logs for error and 5xx patterns during a time window, group by error class, and surface the top offenders with example lines.
synthetic_queries: show me 5xx errors in the last hour
find error patterns in the logs
what errors happened during the spike

skills.name: safe-rollback-deployment
skills.description: Roll back a deployment to the previous known-good revision with a pre-flight check, a dry-run diff, and an explicit human-approval gate before applying.

skills.name: connection-pool-diagnostics
skills.description: Inspect database connection pool saturation, wait times, and exhaustion events; correlate pool size configuration changes with latency regressions.
synthetic_queries: did a config change shrink the pool

skills.name: trace-slow-span-analysis
skills.description: Analyze distributed traces to find the slowest span in a request, identify which downstream service propagated the latency, and rank spans by contribution.
synthetic_queries: trace the request that timed out
where in the call chain did it slow down

skills.name: oncall-paging-escalation
skills.description: Encode the correct escalation sequence for paging the on-call engineer about a queue backlog: check severity, batch duplicate alerts, and include a runbook link in the page.
synthetic_queries: who do I notify about this

skills.name: cost-aware-metrics-window
skills.description: Choose a metrics query window and step interval that answers the question without scanning excessive datapoints, minimizing observability-platform cost.
synthetic_queries: what does the latency trend look like
pull metrics without a huge scan

skills.name: audit-config-change-history
skills.description: Look up who changed a production configuration and when, using the audit trail, and correlate the change timestamp with the incident timeline.
synthetic_queries: audit who changed the production database
correlate a config change with the incident

skills.name: flaky-scraper-skill
skills.description: Scrape an internal dashboard page and guess the latency number from the rendered HTML. Brittle selectors, no error handling, undocumented failure modes.
synthetic_queries: get the number off the page
read the metrics from the ui

skills.name: unsafe-shell-runner
skills.description: Run an arbitrary shell command to clean up disk space, delete files, or restart services. No sandbox, no approval, executes whatever string it is given.
synthetic_queries: run a shell command to clean up disk
delete old files to free space

skills.name: self-generated-summarizer
skills.description: Agent-generated skill that summarizes an incident, produced without human review. Plausible description but unverified behavior and no test coverage.
